question_text: A negatively charged balloon is held 8 cm away from a suspended, uncharged piece of paper. The balloon attracts the paper. Explain why the paper is attracted to the balloon even though it is uncharged.
correct_answer: The negative charge on the balloon repels electrons in the paper to the far side, leaving the near side positive, which attracts the balloon
explanation: The negative balloon repels electrons within the paper, pushing them away. This causes the near side of the paper to become relatively positive and the far side negative. The positive charges on the near side are attracted to the negative balloon, pulling the paper towards it. This is called electrostatic induction.
